question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
evidence: Phenotypic susceptibility to raltegravir was determined using PhenoSense® Integrase. Briefly, integrase sequences were amplified from patient plasma by RT-PCR and transferred into a resistance test vector containing a luciferase reporter gene. Pseudotyped virus containing patient-derived integrase was used to infect HEK293 cells in the presence and absence of serial dilutions of raltegravir. Susceptibility was reported as the fold-change in IC50 (ratio of IC50 of the sample virus to the IC50 of a reference strain [NL4-3]). In addition, replication capacity was determined and expressed as the percent of viral infectivity as measured by luciferase production, in the absence of drug compared to a reference strain (NL4-3).
rationale: The paper mentions that phenotypic susceptibility to raltegravir was determined using PhenoSense® Integrase, and it describes the method used to assess drug susceptibility.

question: What clinical specimens were sequenced?
evidence: We identified 29 individuals who exhibited evidence of incomplete viral suppression on a regimen containing an integrase inhibitor (23 raltegravir, 6 elvitegravir). Prior to initiating the integrase inhi­bitor-based regimen, the median CD4+ T cell count and plasma HIV RNA levels were 62 cells/mm3 and 4.65 log10 copies/mL, respectively. Integrase genotypic and phenotypic resistance testing was performed prior to integrase inhibitor therapy in 45/79 subjects; the median fold-change in IC50 to raltegravir was 0.89. The median replicative capacity was 114%. Four of 45 subjects had a secondary integrase inhibitor associated mutation at baseline (1 subject with E138K, 3 subjects with V151I)10; there were no primary integrase mutations.
rationale: The paper mentions that plasma HIV RNA levels were measured, and the integrase sequences were amplified from patient plasma by RT-PCR.

question: Were any sequences obtained from individuals with virological failure on a treatment regimen?
evidence: We identified 29 individuals who exhibited evidence of incomplete viral suppression on a regimen containing an integrase inhibitor (23 raltegravir, 6 elvitegravir).
